skill.name: backend-orchestrator
skill.description: Coordinates backend API, database, service, integration, and testing work through structured planning and quality gates.
skill.summary: Guides backend delivery from requirements through API, database, service, and test validation.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found Markdown formatting and backend terminology rather than executable behavior. No prompt injection, credential access, network calls, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from broad backend workflow guidance.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Patterns
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution findings are Markdown code fences and inline names for skills or files. They do not execute commands and do not contain shell instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown examples or inline code labels in a documentation file. There is no script, interpreter directive, or command invocation in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches come from ordinary words and documentation labels, including description, Design, REST, and api-design.md. No cryptographic algorithm is configured or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is backend planning vocabulary, not crypto code or security configuration. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Patterns
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance findings are generic task validation language such as tracking progress, API test status, and anti-pattern documentation. They do not instruct host or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe software delivery checks and design anti-patterns. No evidence found of commands such as port scans, host discovery, or system enumeration.

content.value_statement: Backend work often fails when API, database, service, and test tasks are planned separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured backend workflow with explicit quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Breaks backend requests into API, database, service, integration, and testing workstreams.
Defines context packages for specialized backend skills and downstream implementation tasks.
Applies API, database, security, and test checklists before considering work complete.
Coordinates frontend, data, and documentation handoffs for backend changes.
Tracks success metrics such as response time, test coverage, query time, and error rate.
limitations: Does not include executable code, scripts, or automation files.
Does not implement APIs or migrations without an agent applying the guidance.
Assumes project standards such as backend-standard.md and api-design.md exist elsewhere.
Does not replace human review for production security, performance, or data migrations.

use_cases.title: Plan a New REST API
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to define endpoints, data models, service logic, and required tests before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Coordinate a Database Change
use_cases.description: Map schema changes, migration testing, rollback planning, index updates, and documentation tasks in one backend plan.

use_cases.title: Validate a Service Integration
use_cases.description: Organize business logic, external service requirements, authentication checks, and contract tests for an integration release.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Backend Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-orchestrator skill to plan this backend task. Include API changes, database work, service logic, tests, and documentation updates.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Skill Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for delegating work to specialized backend skills.
prompt_templates.prompt: Package this backend task for the right specialized skills. Include current endpoints, data model assumptions, standards, dependencies, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Review Backend Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before implementation or merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend change against the API, database, security, test, and performance checklists. Identify missing work and release risks.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Cross-Team Delivery
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request involving frontend, data, and documentation handoffs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Orchestrate this backend release across frontend contracts, data dependencies, service integration, human documentation, test evidence, and operational metrics.

output_examples.input: Plan an endpoint for product search with filtering and pagination.
output_examples.output: A backend task plan covering endpoint shape, query model, indexes, service responsibilities, validation rules, API tests, load tests, and documentation updates.

output_examples.input: Check whether a database migration is ready for release.
output_examples.output: A readiness review covering schema design, rollback plan, backup verification, index impact, integrity checks, test coverage, and performance risk.
A delivery outline covering authentication, service boundaries, error handling, third-party failure modes, contract tests, security checks, and release metrics.

best_practices: Give the skill current API contracts, schema details, service dependencies, and relevant project standards.
Ask for explicit success criteria before implementation begins.
Use the checklists as review gates before merging backend changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only security review for sensitive production systems.
Do not skip database rollback and backup planning for migrations.
Do not treat the generated plan as implementation without tests and code review.

faq.question: Does this skill write backend code?
faq.answer: It provides orchestration guidance. An AI agent or developer must still implement and test the code.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no executable code, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static analysis produced false positives from Markdown formatting and backend terminology.

faq.question: What standards does it expect?
faq.answer: It references backend-standard.md and api-design.md as project quality guides.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when backend work spans APIs, databases, services, tests, and documentation.
